text: Tisagenlecleucel produced an 81% overall remission rate within 3 months.
paper_title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia
scope_conditions: CD19+ relapsed or refractory B-cell ALL
evidence_span: The overall remission rate within 3 months was 81%

text: All responders were negative for minimal residual disease by flow cytometry.
paper_title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia
evidence_span: all patients who had a response to treatment found to be negative for minimal residual disease, as assessed by means of flow cytometry

text: Event-free and overall survival were reported at 6 and 12 months.
paper_title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia
evidence_span: The rates of event-free survival and overall survival were 73% (95% confidence interval [CI], 60 to 82) and 90% (95% CI, 81 to 95), respectively, at 6 months and 50% (95% CI, 35 to 64) and 76% (95% CI, 63 to 86) at 12 months.

text: Tisagenlecleucel persistence was observed in blood for as long as 20 months.
paper_title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia
evidence_span: Persistence of tisagenlecleucel in the blood was observed for as long as 20 months.

text: Grade 3 or 4 adverse events suspected to be related to tisagenlecleucel occurred in 73% of patients.
paper_title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia
evidence_span: Grade 3 or 4 adverse events that were suspected to be related to tisagenlecleucel occurred in 73% of patients.

text: Cytokine release syndrome occurred in 77% of patients.
paper_title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia
evidence_span: The cytokine release syndrome occurred in 77% of patients, 48% of whom received tocilizumab.

text: Neurologic events occurred in 40% of patients and no cerebral edema was reported.
paper_title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia
evidence_span: Neurologic events occurred in 40% of patients and were managed with supportive care, and no cerebral edema was reported.

text: Tisagenlecleucel was generated from autologous T cells transduced with a lentiviral vector expressing CAR signaling domains.
paper_title: Tisagenlecleucel in Children and Young Adults with B-Cell Lymphoblastic Leukemia
evidence_span: Tisagenlecleucel was generated ex vivo with the use of autologous T cells transduced with a lentiviral vector to express a CAR containing a CD3-zeta domain to provide a T-cell activation signal and a 4-1BB (CD137) domain to provide a costimulatory signal.
